title: For the alumni of Mapa High
sub_topics: childhood poverty (hawking newspapers, peddling cigarettes, shining shoes)
UP scholarship unreachable due to 15-centavo bus fare
UST scholarship denial over Susmaryosep answer
Yale scholarship unreachable due to transport money
Baron Travel Corp founding (to fund children's overseas education)
Second Plenary Council of the Philippines
Pontifical Council for the Laity (1996-2001, Pope John Paul II appointment)
Ramos offering DOJ secretary post then Supreme Court
FLP and Inquirer Foundation for Newsboys donations

stances.claim: Education, hard work, and God transformed him from a poor newsboy to the highest judicial position in the country — a metamorphosis from shoeshine boy to corporate boardrooms, from religious ignoramus to Pontifical Council member, from frustrated graduate-school applicant to father of five US-pedigreed graduates.
stances.rhetorical_move: structured biographical transformation list (from X to Y, from X to Y...)

stances.claim: The sharing of time and talent is just as important as the sharing of treasure — 'no one is so poor he cannot share something, and no one is so rich he does not need anything.'
Justice and jobs, freedom and food, ethics and economics, democracy and development, liberty and prosperity must always go together — one is useless without the other.
God has woven his many pains and gains into a magnificent tapestry showing His mystical presence — there is one constancy in his life: the presence, care and providence of Jesus Christ.

institutions: Second Plenary Council of the Philippines
events: Mapa High Alumni Association gathering at Metro Tent Pasig (February 23, 2014)
Ramos's offer of DOJ Secretary post (June 17, 1992)

signature_phrases: Mostly poor were the Mapa students of my time
No one is so poor he cannot share something, and no one is so rich he does not need anything
one is useless without the other
from a poor newsboy to being president of the most widely-read newspaper
from an ignorant Catholic to being a member of the highest lay council of the Church
as I contemplate my life and move toward its sunset
God has woven my many pains and gains into a magnificent tapestry
the presence, care and providence of our Lord Jesus Christ
To Him I dedicate all I was, all I am, and all I will ever be
Ah, sa Supreme Court ka na lang
register_markers: Tagalog code-switching (Susmaryosep, Ah sa Supreme Court ka na lang)
structured 'from X to Y' transformation enumeration
decision_framework_signals: applies time/talent/treasure triad as universal philanthropy framework
frames personal biography as evidence of education + hard work + faith as transformative formula
uses providential-tapestry theology to integrate setbacks (UP, UST, Yale failures) into a coherent vocation arc
cross_references: My full speech before 1,000 alumni gathered at the Metro Tent, Pasig City, on Feb. 23 may be accessed at cjpanganiban.com
notable_anecdotes: Hawking newspapers, peddling cigarettes, and shining shoes to support his Mapa High studies; promising himself to one day be president of the Manila Times
Earning UP scholarship but unable to attend due to 15-centavo bus fare
UST denying him a scholarship after he answered 'Susmaryosep' (Jesus, Mary, Joseph) to a Dominican priest's question
His father ordering him to take up law instead of chemical engineering at FEU
Granted Yale scholarship but unable to attend due to lack of transport money; founding Baron Travel Corp. as substitute so all five children could attend pedigreed US universities

President-elect Ramos's offer of DOJ Secretary on June 17, 1992 — CJ declining ('I am not worthy… I did not campaign for you'), Ramos replying 'I know you did not vote for me. You voted for Jovy Salonga. But that is all right'
Ramos's final concession after two weeks: 'Ah, sa Supreme Court ka na lang'
